backlog.title: Create written guide / blog post for Chirp programming
backlog.detail: Supplement the video with a written walkthrough — not video-only

backlog.title: Update Zero to Hero Chirp programming tutorial
backlog.detail: Re-record/update the tutorial for current Chirp interface. OUTLINE: Equipment needed: Windows laptop, USB programming cable (FTDI chipset), USB-A to USB-C OTG adapter, recommend 2 cables (Kenwood plug is fragile). Process: (1) Pull original file off radio and save as backup, (2) Duplicate to create working copy, (3) Build out using provided template — copy/paste and modify values, (4) Import repeaters from RepeaterBook, (5) Write to radio. Steps: Download from chirpmyradio.com (use ad blocker, go direct not via Google). Install Chirp Next EXE. Connect radio: set to inactive channel, remove antenna, plug cable firmly, connect USB (should hear Windows ding, no drivers needed). Radio > Download from Radio: select USB Serial Port (not Bluetooth), Vendor=Baofeng, Model=UV-5R (UV-5R Plus is same). Other radios check Chirp site for compatible models (e.g. AR-152 uses BF-8FHP). Progress bar + blinking light = working. Troubleshoot: power cycle, replug, try backup cable. Save original, duplicate, modify with template, import repeaters, write to radio.
